name: Bibingka
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the mochi rice and drain it.
2. Cook the rice in a rice cooker or large saucepan with water.
3. In a saucepan, combine coconut milk and 1 1/4 cups of brown sugar. Heat over medium heat for about 20 minutes, stirring constantly until thickened.
4. Wilt banana leaves over low heat on an electric surface unit or in the microwave oven, then line a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
5. Preheat the electric oven to 350°F.
6. Stir the remaining brown sugar into the hot rice and mix well. Reserve half of the coconut milk mixture.
7. Combine the reserved coconut milk with the remaining brown sugar in the rice.
8. Transfer the mixture into the prepared baking pan.
9. Pour the reserved coconut milk over the rice.
10. Bake for 20 minutes, then broil for 5 minutes to brown the top.
11. Cool and cut into serving pieces. Makes 45 servings.
(Reprinted with permission from The Electric Kitchen & Hawaiian Electric Company)
